Main Finding: Primary diagnostic pair is numeric + LightGBM + target_price.
Risk: Global metrics can hide segment bias.
Recommended Next Action: Tune LightGBM + target_price first with segment-aware validation.

Main Finding: LightGBM/log-target and CatBoost variants are retained as secondary benchmarks.
Risk: A tiny MAE gap can cause over-selection of log-target models if RMSE/R2/segment behavior is ignored.
Recommended Next Action: Compare all four candidate pairs again after tuning.

Main Finding: Bias varies by price segment for the primary diagnostic pair.
Risk: A globally strong model may fail in Low or Premium segments.
Recommended Next Action: Use Low and Premium segment metrics as model-selection constraints.

Main Finding: Premium segment shows underprediction if mean residual is positive and prediction ratio is below 1.
Risk: High-price misses can dominate RMSE and reduce trust in premium estimates.
Recommended Next Action: Test premium sample weighting and feature engineering for rare high-price configurations.

Main Finding: Low segment shows overprediction if mean residual is negative and overprediction rate is high.
Risk: Low-price MAPE can become large even when absolute errors look modest.
Recommended Next Action: Track Low MAPE and overprediction rate during tuning.

Main Finding: Error magnitude may change with actual price.
Risk: RMSE may overweight expensive laptops and understate low-price relative error.
Recommended Next Action: Evaluate sample weighting and segment-aware objectives.

Main Finding: Log-target compression is assessed only as a secondary target-strategy check.
Risk: Log target may reduce MAE/MAPE while compressing premium predictions.
Recommended Next Action: Keep log_target_price for comparison after tuning, not as the primary diagnostic pair.

Main Finding: Extreme errors and prediction ratios were flagged for manual review.
Evidence: Generated 2 potential outlier or error-case CSV files.
Risk: Unverified data issues can distort tuning decisions.
Recommended Next Action: Manually review flagged rows before excluding any observations.

Main Finding: Segment corrections are exploratory and test-set optimistic.
Risk: Applying corrections learned on test data overstates true performance.
Recommended Next Action: Validate calibration with cross-validation or a separate validation split.
